sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after UP
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

changed_rows = []
for i in range(len(before)):
    if before[i] != after[i]:
        changed_rows.append(i)
        
print(f"Changed rows: {changed_rows}")
for r in changed_rows:
    print(f"Row {r}:")
    print(f"  Before: {before[r][16:56]}")
    print(f"  After:  {after[r][16:56]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The UP action changed the first character in the pink panel. The character at the leftmost position changed - it looks like it's cycling through different characters. Let me look at the pink panel characters more carefully. The white marker is positioned above the first character in the pink panel.
So the mechanics seem to be:
- LEFT/RIGHT moves the white marker cursor horizontally
- UP/DOWN changes the character at the cursor position (cycles through options)
The goal is probably to match the characters in the lower panels to the characters in the upper 3×4 grid.

# Let me first understand the character positions in the pink panel

char_starts = [18, 25, 32, 39, 46]

print("=== Pink Panel Characters (current) ===")
for ci, cs in enumerate(char_starts):
    print(f"Char {ci}:")
    for r in range(52, 57):
        print(f"  {ascii_lines[r][cs:cs+6]}")
    print()

# Also check the blue panel
print("=== Blue Panel Characters ===")
for ci, cs in enumerate(char_starts):
    print(f"Char {ci}:")
    for r in range(41, 46):
        print(f"  {ascii_lines[r][cs:cs+6]}")
    print()
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
